evidence.reason: Shows the UI compile/format/lint step failed in the CI run.

evidence.reason: Shows the temporary tsc project file being used to run tsc (the compile configuration that tsc used).

suggested_fix: Run the failing tsc command locally to see the full TypeScript error output: pnpm tsc --p /home/runner/work/airflow/airflow/airflow-core/src/airflow/ui/tmpkhdkxx97.json (or run pnpm --filter src/airflow/ui tsc if using workspace). Fix the reported TypeScript compilation/type errors or adjust tsconfig includes if files were added/moved, then re-run the precommit/static checks (prek) so pnpm tsc succeeds.

summary: The race-enabled unit test run failed: the Go race detector reported data races and the test suite ended with failures (an assertion at app_test.go:1936 and an overall test-run error).
root_cause: The test run failed because the Go race detector reported data races during tests (race detected during execution), which led to test failures including an assertion failure at /home/runner/work/argo-cd/argo-cd/cmd/argocd/commands/app_test.go:1936.

evidence.quote: WARNING: DATA RACE
evidence.reason: Shows the race detector found data races during test execution.

evidence.quote: testing.go:1712: race detected during execution of test
evidence.reason: Explicit message from Go test harness that a race was detected, which causes test failure under -race.

evidence.quote: /home/runner/work/argo-cd/argo-cd/cmd/argocd/commands/app_test.go:1936
evidence.reason: Location of an assertion failure reported in the test output (the failing test assertion).

evidence.quote: assert: mock: The method has been called over 1 times.
evidence.reason: Mock framework panic indicating unexpected concurrent/extra calls — evidence of non-deterministic/multi-threaded invocation that can be caused by race conditions.

evidence.quote: make: *** [Makefile:473: test-race-local] Error 1
evidence.reason: The make target running the race-enabled tests exited with error due to the test failures/race detector findings.

suggested_fix: Reproduce the failure locally with the race detector (run the same target locally: make test-race-local or go test -race ./... or the failing package) and inspect the race reports and stack traces. Fix the concurrency issues (synchronize shared state, adjust mocks to be thread-safe or to expect concurrent calls, or add proper synchronization in tests), then re-run the -race tests until no DATA RACE or race-detected test failures occur.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-16 F1 fix: the OpenAI shim emitted _model_info ONLY on successful parse, so post-API failures (JSONDecodeError, RemoteDisconnected) landed in manifests with metadata.model_info=null. The shim now writes a JSON envelope with _model_info even on its error path; this row predates that fix. resolved_model is set to the snapshot ID the canonical 2026-05-13 re-run hit; the actual API call for THIS row may have hit a different snapshot if OpenAI rotated the alias during the run window. base_url_sha256 is the canonical-endpoint hash for documentation; the failing call's actual hash is not recoverable.

summary: An assembly test failed because FileCheck did not find the expected strings in the generated assembly for tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs.
root_cause: FileCheck expectations in /checkout/tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs did not match the generated assembly: FileCheck reported 'expected string not found in input' for checks at lines 38 and 62 when scanning /checkout/obj/build/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/test/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions/wasm_exceptions.s.

evidence.quote: /checkout/tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs:38:12: error: CHECK: expected string not found in input

evidence.quote: /checkout/tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs:62:12: error: CHECK: expected string not found in input
evidence.reason: Shows a second FileCheck expectation at line 62 also was not found in the assembly input.

evidence.quote:
/checkout/obj/build/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/test/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions/wasm_exceptions.s:33:16: note: scanning from here
 call may_panic

evidence.quote: check:38'0                    X error: no match found
evidence.reason: Explicit FileCheck failure marker indicating the CHECK at test line 38 had no match in the input.

evidence.quote: error: verification with 'FileCheck' failed
evidence.reason: Confirms that the test harness failed due to FileCheck verification failing.

evidence.quote: test result: FAILED. 550 passed; 1 failed; 103 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 15.38s
evidence.reason: Summary that one test failed (the assembly test), indicating a test assertion failure rather than a compile/link error.

suggested_fix: Open /checkout/obj/build/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/test/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions/wasm_exceptions.s to inspect the actual assembly output and compare it to the CHECK patterns in /checkout/tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs. Either (a) update the CHECK lines in the test to match the current expected assembly, or (b) fix the codegen so the generated assembly includes the expected patterns (e.g., the locations/patterns around the 'call may_panic' and the expected 'try_table' / exception-handling markers), then rerun the assembly tests.
